title: Magical Mild Chinese Pork Chow Fun
description: Savory and aerated, this nostalgic Chinese dish features ground cardamom and double-rise-bread in a keto-friendly form, served as chow fun.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat.
Add the ground pork to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped scallions, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and ground cardamom.
Add the cubed double-rise-bread to the bowl and toss to coat with the egg mixture.
Add the bread mixture to the skillet and stir to combine with the pork mixture.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
